title: Data aggregation
abstract: Methods and systems for data modelling. One method includes receiving a data stream including a first plurality of data points, aggregating the first plurality of data points to a second plurality of data points including values at a first frequency, and building, with at least one electronic processor, a first model based on the second plurality of data points, wherein the first model is configured to generate data values at the first frequency. The method also includes accessing a second model based on a third plurality of data points, wherein the second model is configured to generate data values at a second frequency shorter than the first frequency, generating a first data output using the first model, generating a second data output using the second model, multiplying the first data output by the second data output to generate a third data output, and outputting the third data output for display.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of data modelling, the method comprising: receiving a data stream, wherein the data stream comprises a first plurality of data points; aggregating the first plurality of data points to a second plurality of data points, wherein the second plurality of data points include values at a first frequency; building, with at least one electronic processor, a first model based on the second plurality of data points, the first model configured to generate data values at the first frequency; accessing a second model based on a third plurality of data points, the second model configured to generate data values at a second frequency shorter than the first frequency; generating a first data output using the first model; generating a second data output using the second model; multiplying the first data output by the second data output to generate a third data output; and outputting the third data output for display. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the first plurality of data points includes values at varying intervals. 3. The method of any one of claims 1 or 2, wherein the first plurality of data points includes item prices. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the second model represents demand for the item. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the first plurality of data points includes item prices for a first location. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the second model is generated based on demand for the item for the first location and a second location. 7. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the first model is configured to generate a daily sales forecast for an item at a proposed price for a proposed day. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the second model is configured to generate an intra-day demand for the item at the proposed price. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the third data output includes an estimated sales volume at the proposed price value for a pricing period occurring within the proposed day. 10. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising estimating the second frequency using a Gaussian-Uniform mixture model fitted to a set of data points using an expectation- maximisation algorithm. 1 1 . The method of any preceding claim, wherein the second frequency is irregular. 12. A system for data modelling, the system comprising: at least one electronic processor configured to receive a data stream, wherein the data stream comprises a first plurality of data points, aggregate the first plurality of data points to a second plurality of data points, wherein the second plurality of data points include values at a first frequency, build a first model based on the second plurality of data points, the first model configured to generate data values at the first frequency, access a second model based on a third plurality of data points, the second model configured to generate data values at a second frequency shorter than the first frequency, generate a first data output using the first model, generate a second data output using the second model, multiply the first data output by the second data output to generate a third data output, and output the third data output for display. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the first plurality of data points includes values at varying intervals. 14. The system of any one of claims 12 or 13, wherein the first plurality of data points includes item prices and the second model represents demand for the item. 15. The system of claim 14, wherein the second model is generated based on demand for the item for the first location and a second location. 16. The system of any one of claims 12 to 15, further comprising estimating the second frequency using a Gaussian-Uniform mixture model fitted to a set of data points using an expectation-maximisation algorithm. 17. Non-transitory computer-readable medium storing instructions executable by at least one electronic processor to perform a set of functions, the set of functions comprising: receiving a data stream, wherein the data stream comprises a first plurality of data points; aggregating the first plurality of data points to a second plurality of data points, wherein the second plurality of data points include values at a first frequency; building a first model based on the second plurality of data points, the first model configured to generate data values at the first frequency; accessing a second model based on a third plurality of data points, the second model configured to generate data values at a second frequency shorter than the first frequency; generating a first data output using the first model; generating a second data output using the second model; multiplying the first data output by the second data output to generate a third data output; and outputting the third data output for display. 18. The non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 17, wherein the first plurality of data points includes values at varying intervals. 19. The non-transitory computer readable medium of any one of claims 17 or 18, wherein the first plurality of data points includes item prices and the second model represents demand for the item, the first model is configured to generate a daily sales forecast for the item at a proposed price for a proposed day, the second model is configured to generate an intra-day demand for the item at the proposed price, and the third data output includes an estimated sales volume at the proposed price value for a pricing period occurring within the proposed day. 20. The non-transitory computer readable medium of any one of claims 17 to 19, wherein the set of functions further comprises estimating the second frequency using a Gaussian- Uniform mixture model fitted to a set of data points using an expectation-maximisation algorithm.
